suppliers.context: Pursuant to the terms of a manufacturing agreement between both parties, VIS was obligated to use its best commercial efforts to manufacture wafers at specified yield rates for us up to a fixed amount of reserved capacity per month.

suppliers.entity: Systems on Silicon Manufacturing Company Pte. Ltd.
suppliers.context: SSMC’s business is limited to manufacturing wafers for us, our subsidiaries, NXP and NXP’s subsidiaries. While we, together with NXP, have the right to purchase up to 100% of SSMC’s annual capacity, we and NXP are required to purchase, in the aggregate, at least 70% of SSMC’s full capacity.

In January 2007, we acquired a 51.2% equity interest in Xintec, a supplier of wafer level packaging service, to support our CMOS image sensor manufacturing business. In 2024, we incurred total manufacturing expenses of NT$5,233 million (US$160 million) from Xintec.

customers.context: We count among our customers many of the world’s leading semiconductor companies... including, but not limited to, Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.

In 2024, we had total sales of NT$14,089 million (US$430 million) to GUC, representing 0.5% of our total revenue.

single_source_dependencies.context: Also, since we procure some of our raw materials from sole-sourced suppliers, there is a risk that our need for such raw materials may not be met or that back-up supplies may not be readily available.

geographic_concentration.context: The majority of our principal executive officers and our principal production facilities are located in the R.O.C., and the majority of our net revenue is derived from our operations in the R.O.C.
Our corporate headquarters and nine of our fabs are located in the Hsinchu Science Park.

capacity_constraints.context: During periods of decreased demand, certain manufacturing lines or tools in some of our manufacturing facilities may be suspended or shut down temporarily. However, if demand subsequently increases rapidly over a short period of time, we may not be able to restore the capacity in a timely manner to take advantage of the upturn.

supply_chain_risks.context: Also, any increase in the use of export control restrictions and sanctions to target certain countries and entities... could impact not only our ability to continue supplying products to those customers, but also our customers’ demand for our products, and could even lead to changes in semiconductor supply chains.

In the past, shortages in the supply of some materials, whether by specific suppliers or by the semiconductor industry generally, have resulted in occasional industry-wide price adjustments and delivery delays.

We have occasionally suffered power outages, dips or surges caused by difficulties encountered by our electricity supplier or other power consumers on the same power grid. Some of these incidents have resulted in interruptions to our operations.

revenue_concentration.context: Customer A accounted for 22% of net revenue in 2024.
Customer B accounted for 12% of net revenue in 2024.

While we generate revenue from hundreds of customers worldwide, our ten largest customers in 2022, 2023 and 2024 accounted for approximately, 68%, 70% and 76% of our net revenue in the respective year.

geographic_revenue.context: North America accounted for 70% of net revenue in 2024.
China accounted for 11% of net revenue in 2024.
Asia Pacific accounted for 10% of net revenue in 2024.
Japan accounted for 5% of net revenue in 2024.
EMEA accounted for 4% of net revenue in 2024.

purchase_obligations.obligation_type: Capital Purchase or Other Purchase Obligations
purchase_obligations.context: Represents commitments for construction or purchase of equipment, raw material and other property or services.
Represents advance temporary receipts from customer... to retain specified capacity at our fabs.

market_risk_disclosures.exposure: NT dollar against U.S. dollar, Euro, and Japanese yen
market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: every 1% depreciation of the U.S. dollar against the NT dollar would result in an approximately 0.4 percentage point decrease in our operating margin
market_risk_disclosures.hedging: foreign currency derivatives contracts, such as currency forwards or currency swaps, and non-derivative financial instruments, such as foreign currency denominated debts
market_risk_disclosures.context: Substantially all of our sales are denominated in U.S. dollars and over half of our capital expenditures are denominated in currencies other than the NT dollar, primarily in U.S. dollars, Euros and Japanese yen.

market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: hypothetical adverse interest rate change of 100 basis points across all maturities would have decreased our other comprehensive income by NT$4,501 million
market_risk_disclosures.hedging: interest rate derivatives to partially hedge interest rate risk on our fixed income investments and anticipated debt issuance
market_risk_disclosures.context: We are exposed to interest rate risks primarily in relation to our investment portfolio and outstanding debt.
